summary: Rust hybrid generic PerimeterX (HUMAN Security) cookie solver. Runs as a REST service that, given a target URL, returns a valid _px3 (and companion) cookie bundle plus matching User-Agent. Uses a chromiumoxide-driven Chromium pool as primary solver (browser harvester), DashMap + optional Redis as TTL cookie cache, and a stub for future native sensor-payload generation. Generic: detects PX appId at runtime from window._pxAppId / first-party /<appId>/init.js path.

context.problem: PX/HUMAN protects many sites with first-party reverse-proxy challenges. Real research, defensive testing, and authorized scraping workflows need a reliable way to obtain valid PX cookies without re-implementing the full stealth pipeline in every project.
primary_use_cases: Authorized scraping of allowlisted public-data sites
Defensive security research / red-team training labs
CTF and educational reverse-engineering of bot-mitigation stacks
out_of_scope_use_cases: Account takeover, credential stuffing, or any form of unauthorized access
Bypassing access controls on private/authenticated content
DoS or high-volume abuse of target sites
Reselling solver capacity to anonymous third parties

stack.architecture_standard: axum-best-practice (Clean Architecture, max 200 LOC/file, AppError, repository pattern)

deliverable_themes: Cargo workspace scaffold conforming to axum-best-practice
PX detection module (appId extraction, gate-cookie taxonomy)
Browser harvester pool with manual CDP stealth patches
Cookie cache layer (TTL, Redis adapter trait)
REST API with API-key auth + per-domain allowlist + audit log

constraints.timeline: Iterative; no hard deadline. MVP target: 4 weeks part-time.
compliance: No PII storage beyond ephemeral solver cookies
Audit log every solve request (target domain, requester key id, outcome, latency)
Refuse solves for sites whose ToS the operator has not opted into (manual config)
constraints.hosting: Self-hosted; assumed Linux x86_64 with Chromium available.

success_metrics.canary_solve_rate: >= 95% over 100 consecutive solves against pedidosya.com.ar from clean IP

risks: PX bumps init.js obfuscation → harvester may need stealth patch updates
IP reputation block on shared hosts → proxy support required for production
Dual-use abuse → API-key allowlist + audit log + ToS/legal docs
Chromium memory pressure → worker pool sizing + restart policy
